vulkanmod.options.performancePreset.tool: Applies a group of Minecraft and VulkanMod settings for the selected performance target.
vulkanmod.options.advCulling.tooltip: Use a culling algorithm that might improve performance by reducing the number of non visible chunk sections rendered.
vulkanmod.options.ao.subBlock.tooltip: ON (Sub-block): Enables smooth lighting for non full block (experimental).
vulkanmod.options.entityCulling.tooltip: Enables culling for entities on not visible sections.
vulkanmod.options.frameQueue.tooltip: Higher values might help stabilize frametime but will increase input lag.
vulkanmod.options.indirectDraw.tooltip: Reduces CPU overhead but increases GPU overhead. Enabling it might help in CPU limited systems.
vulkanmod.options.adaptiveChunkUploads.t: Adjusts chunk upload work from recent frame time to reduce stutter while moving or flying.
vulkanmod.options.uniqueOpaqueLayer.tool: Use a unique render layer for opaque terrain to improve performance.
vulkanmod.options.particleCulling.toolti: Cull redundant particles based on distance, player FOV, and maximum same-type count budgets.
vulkanmod.options.blockEntityCulling.too: Cull block entities (chests, signs, spawners, etc.) that are outside the camera frustum or further than 96 blocks.
vulkanmod.options.leavesCulling.tooltip: Cull adjacent interior faces of leaf blocks to significantly improve performance in forests.
vulkanmod.options.renderScale.tooltip: Renders Minecraft at a lower internal resolution, then scales it back to the current window or fullscreen size.
vulkanmod.options.shadersEnabled.tooltip: Enable Volcanic's native Vulkan post-processing effects.
vulkanmod.options.horizonFog.tooltip: Smoothly fades distant terrain into the sky right at the render distance edge to hide chunk borders. Lower values pull the fog closer to the edge and make it thinner; Off disables it entirely.
vulkanmod.options.optimizedShadows.toolt: Render shadows at half resolution for a large performance gain. Slightly softer shadow edges.
vulkanmod.options.disableHiDPI.tooltip: macOS: render at the display's logical resolution instead of the full Retina resolution. Much faster, slightly softer image. Requires restart.
